benchmarks.d1_retention: > 40% is good for social, > 25% for utility
benchmarks.viral_coefficient: > 1.0 for viral growth (each user brings > 1 new user)

best_practices: Optimize Day 1 onboarding — D1 retention is the most actionable metric
Use push notifications for re-engagement, not just promotions
Design social mechanics early — sharing, following, reactions, leaderboards
A/B test onboarding flows continuously — small changes compound
Track event-level retention by acquisition source and first action
Build habit loops: trigger → action → variable reward → investment
Identify and amplify the 'magic moment' — the first time users get core value
Mobile-first design — > 80% of consumer app usage is on mobile
common_risks: D1 retention cliff — users sign up, never return after first session
Notification fatigue — over-sending causes opt-out and eventual uninstall
Feature creep — adding features that don't improve core habit loop
Algorithm dependency for content feeds — content quality degrades at scale
App store policy risk — payments, content moderation, data policies
Privacy regulation risk — GDPR/CCPA for EU and California users
Platform dependency — iOS/Android OS changes breaking core features
growth_patterns: Viral loops — social sharing, invites, referral rewards
Content marketing — SEO from user-generated content (profiles, posts)
Influencer seeding — get 50 power users before 50,000 regular users
Word-of-mouth by design — make the app shareable by default
Mobile app install campaigns via Meta and TikTok ads
Cross-platform presence — iOS, Android, and web for maximum reach

retention_frameworks.d1: Focus on onboarding — user must reach 'magic moment' within first session
retention_frameworks.d7: Focus on habit formation — create a reason to return within 7 days
retention_frameworks.d30: Focus on value depth — user should have unlocked > 3 features or content pieces

viral_loops: Share output loop — user creates something worth sharing (photo, result, score)
Invite friend loop — collaboration or social features require another user
Competitive loop — leaderboard or streak system creates social pressure to return
Content discovery loop — user discovers the app via another user's shared content
recommended_integrations: Amplitude or Mixpanel — event tracking, retention cohorts, funnel analysis
AppsFlyer or Branch — mobile attribution and deep links
